resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def isPowerOfFour(self, n: int) -> bool:
        return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0 and (n & 0x55555555) != 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses bitwise operations to check if the number is a power of four. It first checks if the number is positive, then checks if it's a power of two by verifying that it has exactly one bit set in its binary representation (i.e., n & (n - 1) == 0). Finally, it checks if the least significant bit is set, which is a necessary condition for a power of four.
